title: Anurosperma pervillei
taxonomy: Anurosperma pervillei is a species of flowering plant in the family Restionaceae. It is native to the island of Réunion, which is a French overseas department in the Indian Ocean. The classification of Anurosperma pervillei includes the kingdom Plantae, the clade angiosperms, and the order Poales. It is a type of rush or sedge, characterized by its long, slender stems and narrow leaves. As a member of the Restionaceae family, Anurosperma pervillei is closely related to other plants in this family, such as the genus Restio.
